event_text: The East-West Schism divides Eastern and Western Christianity.
The First Crusade brings large European armies to the eastern Mediterranean.
Magna Carta is sealed in England and influences later constitutional ideas.
The Black Death kills a large share of Europe's population.
Constantinople falls to the Ottoman Empire.
Martin Luther's Ninety-five Theses help begin the Protestant Reformation.
The Thirty Years War devastates much of Central Europe.
The Peace of Westphalia ends the Thirty Years War.
Ottoman forces are defeated at Vienna.
The War of the Spanish Succession reshapes European power politics.
The French Revolution begins.
The Napoleonic Wars transform Europe.
The Congress of Vienna redraws the European order.
Revolutions spread across Europe.
The German Empire is proclaimed at Versailles.
The First World War is fought across Europe and beyond.
The Treaty of Versailles is signed after the First World War.
The Second World War devastates Europe.
The United Nations is founded after the Second World War.
NATO is founded.
The Treaty of Paris creates the European Coal and Steel Community.
The Treaty of Rome creates the European Economic Community.
The Berlin Wall is built.
The Berlin Wall falls.
The Maastricht Treaty is signed, creating the European Union.
Ten countries join the European Union in its largest enlargement.
